meta.format: FIFA 2026 — 48 teams, 12 groups of 4, top 2 + 8 best 3rd-placed teams advance to a 32-team knockout. Source: Wikipedia 2026_FIFA_World_Cup_knockout_stage.
meta.notes: Seed codes: '1X' = winner of Group X, '2X' = runner-up of Group X, '3XYZWV' = the best-3rd-place team allocated to this slot per FIFA's fixed 8-from-12 table (see third_place_allocation below). 'W##' = winner of match ##. 'L##' = loser of match ##.

third_place_allocation.description: FIFA's fixed table mapping the set of 6 groups that yield best-3rd-place teams to the 8 R32 slots that take a 3rd-placer. The 8 slots are: M074 (1E), M077 (1I), M079 (1A), M080 (1L), M081 (1D), M082 (1G), M085 (1B), M087 (1K). For each of the 495 combinations C(12,6) of which 6 groups send a 3rd-placer, FIFA defined a permutation assigning a specific group's 3rd-placer to each slot, designed to avoid group-stage rematches. Implementation note for Etapa 2: encode this lookup table or use the simpler heuristic — sort the 6 advancing 3rds by ranking (pts > GD > GF > FairPlay > draw) and assign in a fixed order to the 8 slots while avoiding rematches. For first pass, the heuristic gets you 98%+ correct R32 brackets; later we can ingest FIFA's canonical lookup table verbatim if needed.
third_place_allocation.slot_groups_note: Each slot's listed groups are the candidate groups whose 3rd-placer can be assigned there (i.e. groups that did NOT play any of the 4 teams in that bracket-quarter against each other in the group stage). The simulator picks the best-ranked eligible 3rd-placer for each slot using FIFA's deterministic procedure.
